title: Senior Executive / Associate (Conservancy Management)
description:
The Job

Conservancy Operations & Vendor Administration

  • Monitor and track vendors’ daily operational submissions (work schedules, checklists, service reports).
  • Review and verify vendors’ monthly performance reports against contractual requirements.
  • Conduct regular and ad-hoc site walkabouts and document observations for Manager’s review.
  • Follow up with vendors on rectification of service lapses and non-compliances.
  • Check supporting evidence for completed works and identify any non-performance or discrepancies.
  • Prepare service lapse and deduction summaries for Manager’s endorsement.
  • Maintain proper records of completed maintenance and conservancy activities.

Site Inspection & Work Supervision

  • Carry out scheduled and ad-hoc site inspections to monitor cleanliness, maintenance conditions, and work quality.
  • Supervise on-site conservancy works to ensure adherence to approved scope, service standards, and safety requirements.
  • Highlight defects, safety issues, or non-compliances and escalate where necessary.

Feedback & Complaint Management

  • Receive, coordinate, and follow up on feedback, complaints, and service requests related to conservancy matters.
  • Liaise with vendors and stakeholders to ensure timely resolution of issues.
  • Track feedback status and update Manager on progress and closure.

Ad-hoc, Emergency & Operational Support

  • Assist in managing ad-hoc tasks, urgent works, and emergency situations affecting conservancy operations.
  • Support Manager in ensuring service continuity during unforeseen events.
  • Prepare incident summaries and follow-up actions as required

Documentation, Reporting & Administrative Support

  • Maintain accurate records, reports, and correspondence related to conservancy operations.
  • Assist in preparation of operational summaries, reports, and data for management review.
  • Support tender and contract administration tasks when required.

The Requirements 

  • Minimum GCE ‘O’ Level with formal qualifications in Facilities, Building or Estate Management will be advantages. 
  • Minimum 8 years of relevant experience in conservancy, facilities, estate, or site-based operations such as site inspection, on-ground supervision, vendor coordination, follow-up works and monitoring of service delivery and rectification of issues.

postedCompany.description:
Headquartered in Singapore, Jurong Port is a leading international multi-purpose port operator handling general, bulk and containerised cargo. Jurong Port began operations in 1965 as a general and bulk cargo port, serving the development needs of Singapore's Jurong Industrial estate. Today, its main gateway terminal in Singapore welcomes more than 17,000 vessels each year from both the region and internationally.

Jurong Port places particular emphasis on talent management. On this journey, we draw upon the strengths of our growing number of staff of diverse nationailities and cultures. In our search for new talent, our focus is on getting the best we can find, from Singapore and the world.
postedCompany.name: JURONG PORT PTE LTD
